title: lagenocarpus minarum
intro: Lagenocarpus minarum, also known as the Brazilian capybara grass or "capivarada," is a type of aquatic plant belonging to the family Cyperaceae. Traditionally used in folk medicine, it is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The plant is said to have various health benefits, such as treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as skin conditions like eczema and acne. In herbal preparations, Lagenocarpus minarum is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and salves, which can be applied topically or ingested to harness its therapeutic benefits.
